---
title: "The Monist Engine"
---

:::{.hero-section style="padding-top: 3rem; padding-bottom: 2rem;"}
:::{.lead}
**The Hardware Layer.** The Monist Engine is a bare-metal, GPU-accelerated logic environment. By abandoning traditional hierarchical type-checking and contextual grammars, it compiles structural typestates directly into lock-free WebGPU Interaction Nets.
:::
:::

## 1. Syntax is Dead: The Geometry Layer

Transitioning to geometric validation demands the eradication of abstract syntax trees (ASTs). Standard parsers rely on Context-Free Grammars (CFGs) and Pushdown Automata, which inherently fail to track W.V.O. Quine’s relative integer type-shifts across nested mathematical sets.

The **Oracle Layer** (`monist-core`) acts as our CPU-bound geometry solver. It transforms raw mathematical expressions into pure topological matrices:

- **Kosaraju's SCC:** Before bounding logic, the system executes Kosaraju’s Strongly Connected Components algorithm. This flattens 0-weight semantic equality rings into representative concept vertices, physically collapsing redundant structural cycles in system RAM.
- **Bellman-Ford Bounds:** The logic is treated as a spatial constraint problem. The solver assigns directed edges based on logical relations. The Bellman-Ford algorithm verifies these constraints, mathematically proving that variables can populate the matrix without generating a negative-weight cycle. 

## 2. Destroying the Variable: The Compiler Layer

Traditional computational architectures treat variables as persistent symbolic references, causing physical memory bottlenecks during deep recursion. The `monist-comb` compiler completely annihilates the lexical environment.

By replacing named variables with **De Bruijn indexing** and pure, untyped combinatory logic ($S, K, I$), the compiler reduces math into structural coordinates. Crucially, the typestate limits generated by the Bellman-Ford layer are burned directly into the physical execution graph as structural $T$-functor wrappers (`Comb.t_inject`). The physics engine uses these to regulate recursion depth natively without tracking variable names.

## 3. Pulling the Plug: Bare-Metal Physics

Classical engines crash via stack-overflow when fed paradoxical loops like Russell's Paradox. 

Monist subverts this through spatial interception. If Bellman-Ford flags a negative-weight cycle (calculating the Minimum Cycle Mean), it intercepts the mathematical impossibility *prior* to hardware scaling. It triggers a hard structural `K_ITERATION_HALT`, freezing paradoxical regression into safely observable geometric boundaries.

Valid graphs are passed to the WebGPU compute shaders, where 32-bit tagged pointers and lock-free Compare-And-Swap (CAS) atomic loops physically collide atomic nodes in massive parallel swarms.

:::{style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 2rem; padding: 2rem; background: #fafafa; border: 1px solid #eaeaea; border-radius: 8px;"}
### The Core Crates
- [`crates/monist-parser`](https://github.com/ryanncode/monist/tree/main/crates/monist-parser): Parses raw text and ASCII constraints.
- [`crates/monist-core`](https://github.com/ryanncode/monist/tree/main/crates/monist-core): The Oracle Layer, executing Kosaraju SCC & Bellman-Ford constraints.
- [`crates/monist-comb`](https://github.com/ryanncode/monist/tree/main/crates/monist-comb): The Interaction Net Backend, hosting the WebGPU (`wgpu`) WGSL compute shaders.
- [`crates/monist-seq`](https://github.com/ryanncode/monist/tree/main/crates/monist-seq): Sequent calculus evaluation bridging structural rules.
- [`crates/monist-psg`](https://github.com/ryanncode/monist/tree/main/crates/monist-psg): Phase Space Geometry and spatial boundary check limits.
- [`crates/monist-verify`](https://github.com/ryanncode/monist/tree/main/crates/monist-verify): The validation gateway connecting to the formal proofs.
:::
